description: SSRF occurs when a web application fetches a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL, allowing attackers to make requests to internal services.

entries.name: SSRF to AWS IMDS (Instance Metadata Service)
entries.description: SSRF vulnerability allows attacker to reach AWS metadata service at 169.254.169.254, stealing IAM role credentials.
entries.how_it_works: Application makes HTTP request to user-controlled URL. Attacker provides http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ROLE_NAME which returns temporary AWS credentials for the instance/Lambda role.
preconditions: Application makes outbound HTTP requests with user-controlled URL
IMDSv1 enabled (or IMDSv2 without hop limit)
impact: Full access to whatever the role permits
affected_components: Any HTTP client with user-controlled URL
entries.detection_guidance: Find paths where user input influences a URL that is fetched server-side. In Lambda: check if any URL is constructed from request body/params.
entries.vulnerable_code:
import requests
url = body.get('webhook_url')
response = requests.get(url)  # User controls URL
remediation: Block requests to internal IP ranges (RFC 1918, link-local, loopback)
Use IMDSv2 with hop limit of 1 (prevents SSRF from containers)
Use VPC endpoints instead of public internet for AWS service access
DNS rebinding protection: resolve hostname, check IP, then fetch

entries.name: SSRF to Internal AWS Services (VPC Endpoints)
entries.description: SSRF allows reaching internal AWS service endpoints not accessible from the internet.
entries.how_it_works: If Lambda/EC2 is in a VPC with access to internal services (Elasticsearch, RDS, Redis), SSRF can reach these services. Attacker queries internal databases or admin panels.
preconditions: Application in VPC with internal service access
affected_components: Any HTTP client in VPC-connected compute
entries.detection_guidance: Find HTTP requests with user-influenced URLs in Lambda/EC2 that run in a VPC. Even if IMDS is protected, internal services may be vulnerable.
entries.vulnerable_code:
# Lambda in VPC fetches user-provided URL
url = event['body']['document_url']
response = requests.get(url)
entries.safe_code:
# Whitelist allowed URL patterns
ALLOWED_DOMAINS = ['s3.amazonaws.com', 'specific-partner.com']
parsed = urlparse(url)
if parsed.hostname not in ALLOWED_DOMAINS:
    raise ValueError('Domain not allowed')
remediation: Use signed URLs for document fetching
Add network segmentation (security groups on internal services)
Require authentication on all internal services

entries.name: Blind SSRF via DNS Rebinding
entries.description: URL validation checks DNS at validation time, but DNS rebinds to internal IP at fetch time.
entries.how_it_works: Attacker's DNS server returns a public IP on first query (passes validation) then returns 169.254.169.254 on second query (during actual fetch). Time-of-check-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerability.
preconditions: URL validation resolves hostname separately from the fetch
impact: Access to IMDS or internal services
affected_components: Any URL validation that resolves DNS separately from fetch
entries.detection_guidance: Check if URL validation resolves hostname independently from the HTTP library's resolution.
exploit_payloads: http://rebind.attacker.com/ (resolves to public IP first, then 169.254.169.254)
entries.vulnerable_code:
# Validate: resolve hostname
ip = socket.gethostbyname(parsed.hostname)  # Returns public IP
assert not is_internal(ip)  # Passes!

# Fetch: library resolves again
response = requests.get(url)  # DNS now returns 169.254.169.254
entries.safe_code:
# Resolve ONCE, then connect to that IP directly
import socket
ip = socket.gethostbyname(parsed.hostname)
if is_internal(ip):
    raise ValueError('Internal IP')
# Force connection to the resolved IP
response = requests.get(url, headers={'Host': parsed.hostname},
                        allow_redirects=False,
                        stream=True)
remediation: Resolve DNS once and pin the result
Use the resolved IP directly for the connection
Disable redirects (attacker can redirect to internal IP)
Use a proxy/firewall that validates destination IPs
